2604.20723.title: Tokenised Flow Matching for Hierarchical Simulation Based Inference
2604.20723.paper_summary: The cost of simulator evaluations is a key practical bottleneck for Simulation Based Inference (SBI). In hierarchical settings with shared global parameters and exchangeable site-level parameters and observations, this structure can be exploited to improve simulation efficiency. Existing hierarchical SBI approaches factorise the posterior yet still simulate across multiple sites per training sample; We instead explore likelihood factorisation (LF) to train from single-site simulations. In LF sampling we learn a per-site neural surrogate of the simulator and then assemble synthetic multi-site observations to amortise inference for the full hierarchical posterior. Building on this, we propose Tokenised Flow Matching for Posterior Estimation (TFMPE), a tokenised flow matching approach that supports function-valued observations through likelihood factorisation. To enable systematic evaluation, we introduce a benchmark for hierarchical SBI. We validate TFMPE on this benchmark and on realistic infectious disease and computational fluid dynamics models, finding well-calibrated posteriors while reducing computational cost.
2604.20723.paper_summary_zh: 模擬器評估的成本是基於模擬推斷 (SBI) 的一個關鍵實際瓶頸。<lb/>在具有共享全局參數和可交換的站點級參數及觀察的分層設置中，可以利用這種結構來提高模擬效率。<lb/>現有的分層SBI方法雖然對後驗進行了因式分解，但仍然在每個訓練樣本中跨多個站點進行模擬；我們則探索了似然因式分解 (LF)，以從單站點模擬中進行訓練。<lb/>在LF取樣中，我們學習每個站點的模擬器神經代理，然後組合合成的多站點觀察，以攤銷整個分層後驗的推斷。<lb/>基於此，我們提出了用於後驗估計的標記流匹配 (TFMPE)，這是一種通過似然因式分解支持函數值觀察的標記流匹配方法。<lb/>為了實現系統評估，我們引入了一個分層SBI的基準。<lb/>我們在這個基準上以及在現實的傳染病和計算流體力學模型上驗證了TFMPE，發現其後驗校準良好，同時降低了計算成本。
2604.20723.author: Giovanni Charles et.al.
2604.20723.authors: Giovanni Charles, Cosmo Santoni, Seth Flaxman, Elizaveta Semenova
